name: py-senertec
documentation:
# py-senertec

[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)
[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/py-senertec.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/py-senertec)

## Description

The **py-senertec** library provides a way to communicate with Senertec Dachsportal2 to monitor your energy unit.
This library supports read-only communication. So *changing* values for your energy unit isn't implemented and not planned yet.

## Requirements

*   **Python 3.9+**
*   **Account for Senertec Dachsportal2/Remeha KWK**

## Tested with this devices

I could test with this devices but others should also work:  
*   Senertec Dachs 0.8
*   Senertec Dachs InnoGen
*   Senertec Dachs Gen2 F5.5
*   Remeha eLecta 300 (technically same as Senertec Dachs 0.8)


## Installation

```sh
$ pip install py-senertec
```

## Usage

### Login and initialization

```python
from senertec.client import senertec
from senertec.canipValue import canipValue
import json
import os

#this example uses no filtering, read below how to use a filter instead of None as first parameter.
senertec = senertec(None)
#set your callback function for messages
senertec.messagecallback = self.output
senertec.login("username", "password")
senertec.init()
```

### Requesting data

```python
units = senertec.getUnits()
senertec.connectUnit(units[0].serial)
# request all available data from all boards
for points in senertec.boards:
            ids = points.getFullDataPointIds()
            # result will be received through callback function which was set above
            senertec.request(ids)
senertec.logout()
```

### Using callback function

Once the websocket has been started, data will be transmitted through the websocket.
To get the websocket data, you need to add a callback which was done above.
The callback function could look like this:

```python
def output(self, value: canipValue):
        print(value.friendlyDataName + ": " +
              value.dataValue.__str__() + value.dataUnit)
```

### Errors of energy unit
Errors can also be read out with a simple function.
The errors are read out on the connect function and will only be refreshed on a reconnect.

```python
# values are returned directly from function
errors = senertec.getErrors()
```

A full example can be found [here](https://github.com/Kleinrotti/py-senertec/blob/main/examples/output_data.py)

## Filtering (recommended)
If you specify a json string in the senertec contructor you can limit what datapoints should be received.
This is pretty usefull if you know what data you want from your heating system e.g. power, temperature.
By default all datapoints are included which are more than 400 in most cases and receiving them takes some time.
This json string should look like [this](https://github.com/Kleinrotti/py-senertec/blob/main/examples/datapointFilter.json).
The json string contains the productGroup at the top and below the datapoints which should be included.
You get the productGroup from the getUnits() function.

## What are these datapoints?
Take a look at this manual from [Remeha](https://mediacdn.remeha.de/-/media/websites/remehade/downloads/produkte/regenerative-hybrid/gas-hybrid-waerme-und-strom/electa-ace-300/electaace300_bedienungsanleitung_02-23.pdf?v=1&d=20230228T114400Z) (Page 39).
There is already a good explanation of how these datapoints are composed.

classes.name: senertec.lang.lang
documentation: Represents all available languages.

classes.name: senertec.canipError.canipError

methods.name: senertec.canipError.canipError.boardName
documentation: Return the board name.

methods.name: senertec.canipError.canipError.counter
documentation: Return how often the error occured.

methods.name: senertec.canipError.canipError.currentError
documentation: Return if the error is currently present.

methods.name: senertec.canipError.canipError.code
documentation: Return the error code.

methods.name: senertec.canipError.canipError.errorTranslation
documentation: Return human readable error message.

methods.name: senertec.canipError.canipError.errorCategory
documentation: Return human readable error category.

methods.name: senertec.canipError.canipError.timestamp
documentation: Return the timestamp when the error happened.
Represents an error of a board.

classes.name: senertec.energyUnit.energyUnit
documentation: Represents an energy unit.

classes.name: senertec.canipValue.canipValue
documentation: Represents a data value received from websocket.

classes.name: senertec.board.board

methods.name: senertec.board.board.datapointCount
documentation: Return number of datapoints.

methods.name: senertec.board.board.datapoints
documentation: Returns all datapoints.

methods.name: senertec.board.board.boardName
documentation: Return the board name.

methods.name: senertec.board.board.friendlyName
documentation: Return the human readable name of the board.

methods.name: senertec.board.board.getFullDatapoint
documentation: Get the full datapoint string.

methods.name: senertec.board.board.getFullDatapointIdByName
documentation:
Get a full datapoint by its name. Not case sensitive.

`name` Datapoint name e.g. BM001

Returns the full datapoint as string or None if not found.

methods.name: senertec.board.board.getDatapointByName
documentation:
Get a datapoint by its name e.g BM001. Not case sensitive.

Returns the datapoint object or None if not found.

methods.name: senertec.board.board.getFullDataPointIds
documentation:
Get all datapoints by its full id.

Returns a list of all datapoints as string. The first part of the string is the board identifier followed
by a unique id which is random on each new session.
Represents an electronic board of the energy unit.

classes.name: senertec.obdClass.obdClass
documentation: This class defines the class/type of a datapoint.

classes.name: senertec.datapoint.datapoint
documentation: Represents a datapoint of a board.

classes.name: senertec.client.basesocketclient
documentation: Base class which provides logic for a senertec websocket connection.

classes.name: senertec.client.senertec

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.__init__
documentation:
Constructor, create instance of senertec client.

`datapointList` Json string to add only these datapoints instead of everything.

`language` Set to your language.

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.boards
documentation: Return all boards.

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.loglevel
documentation: Return the log level.

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.language
documentation: Return or set the language used for sensor names.

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.__parseDataPoints__
documentation: Parse all available datapoints for connected unit.

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.__parseDataPointsFiltered__
documentation: Use this function to include only datapoints of datapointList which was set in class constructor.

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.portalVersion
documentation:
Returns Senertec Dachsportal version.

Init function has to be called first.

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.login
documentation:
Login to senertec platform.

`email` Your email your are registered with.

`password` Your password.

This function needs to be called first.

Returns True on success, False on failure.

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.logout
documentation:
Logout from senertec.

Returns True on success, False on failure.

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.init
documentation:
Initialize Senertec platform and connect to websocket.

This function needs to be called after login.

Returns True on success, False on failure.

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.getUnits
documentation:
Get all units.

Returns all energy units of this account as object list.

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.connectUnit
documentation:
This function connects to a unit and enables receiving data for that unit.

`serial` Serial number of energy unit. Can be received with getUnits() method.

Returns True on success, False on failure.

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.disconnectUnit
documentation:
Disconnect from a unit.

Returns True on success, False on failure.

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.getChart
documentation: Get a history chart of the connected unit.

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.request
documentation:
Request data from specific datapoints of the connected unit.

Data will be received through websocket.

`dataPoints` List of datapoint strings or a single datapoint as string.

Returns True on success, False on failure.

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.getBoardList
documentation: Get all boards of the connected unit

methods.name: senertec.client.senertec.getErrors
documentation:
Get an error history of the connected unit.

`onlyCurrentErrors` Return only errors which are present now. Set to false if you want error history.

This gets loaded when a unit gets connected.
Class to communicate with Senertec and handle network calls
